name: Young Jackfruit and Seaweed Salad

ingredients.item: fresh young green jackfruit or breadfruit, peeled, or canned jackfruit, drained
toasted sesame seeds, plus 1 teaspoon for garnish (optional)
dried, seasoned seaweed crumbs (see Note), plus more to taste
bird’s eye chili pepper, thinly sliced, for garnish (optional)

method: Place the young jackfruit in a pot, add salt, and then boil until soft enough to be easily skewered with a fork or chopstick (20 to 25 minutes for fresh or 15 minutes for canned; if using breadfruit, boil for 10 to 15 minutes). Remove from heat and allow to cool.
Cut jackfruit into thin slices, stack them on top of each other, and then cut along the grain into julienned strips (see Note).

Set a fine-mesh sieve over a heatproof bowl near the stovetop. Heat vegetable oil in a small skillet over medium heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until golden brown (about 2 minutes). Immediately empty contents of skillet into the fine-mesh sieve and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine jackfruit, stock powder, and 1 tablespoon of the fried leek oil. Mix thoroughly until jackfruit absorbs all seasonings.
Add peanuts, sesame seeds, mint, and seasoned seaweed, and mix well. Add more seasoned seaweed to taste.
Transfer salad to plates. Garnish with more sesame seeds and chili slices (if using) and serve with sesame rice crackers (if using).
